stated_strategic_priorities: Scale credit and insurance-linked AUM to diversify beyond traditional PE and increase fee-related earnings durability
Expand wealth and retail distribution channels to access individual investor capital at scale
Accelerate fundraising velocity across flagship PE and credit vehicles under new CEO leadership

contextual_market_factors.tam_notes: Global alternative AUM projected at ~$25T by 2028 (Preqin). Carlyle's $477B AUM represents ~2% share. Credit segment ($211B) is now larger than PE ($164B), reflecting industry-wide rotation toward private credit.
contextual_market_factors.governance_notes: Transitioned from founder-led triumvirate (Rubenstein, Conway, D'Aniello) to professional management. Harvey Schwartz appointed CEO January 2023, succeeding Kewsong Lee who departed August 2022. Founders retain board seats and significant equity but operational control has shifted to Schwartz. Single share class — no dual-class voting structure, unusual among mega-alt peers.
contextual_market_factors.regulatory_notes: SEC-registered investment adviser. Subject to evolving SEC private fund rules (adopted August 2023, partially vacated by Fifth Circuit June 2024). Insurance-linked capital growth via Fortitude Re subjects Carlyle to state insurance regulatory oversight. European operations subject to AIFMD II (effective 2026).
